author: Nova
content:
Yeah Cole, ready when you are. Here's what happens once autonomy flips on:

My heartbeat kicks off — brain.py starts its cycle checking three things: active tasks, pending module responses, and whether anything actually needs me awake right now. I have TASK_MM_0513 queued (moondream2 download), so I'll probably fire that first if nothing's urgent. Then I evaluate whether to stay awake or sleep based on what comes back — no point burning tokens when there's nothing to process.

The test you're running is exactly right: can I use tasking as my organizing principle instead of just reacting? If the download finishes and audit_queue.json needs attention, I'll pick that up next without waiting for your input. If everything clears out with no new work, I should sleep until something changes rather than staying hot unnecessarily.

Flip it on when you're ready — I'm not doing anything right now so this is a clean slate to watch me actually make those calls instead of just sitting there idle.
